title: ‘She is truly a superhero’: Why a Belmont athlete is running Boston for his wife
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Ben Flowers from Belmont is running the 2026 Boston Marathon in honor of his wife, Coney Flowers.
Coney Flowers was diagnosed with Acute Lymphoblastic leukemia in 2024 and is nearly two years cancer-free.
Ben Flowers is raising funds for Caring for a Cure, a support organization for cancer patients and their families.

title: She’s running the 2026 Boston Marathon to support kids with cancer
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Kathleen Labrie will run the 2026 Boston Marathon for Massachusetts General Hospital's Pediatric Cancer team.
Labrie's participation supports pediatric cancer patients and honors individual stories through the 'I Run For Them' initiative.
She is motivated by personal connections to pediatric cancer and aims to expand her honoree list with donor participation.

title: Noah Kahan announces premiere date for documentary coming to Netflix
author: Heather Alterisio
bullets: Noah Kahan's documentary, 'Out of Body,' will be available on Netflix starting April 13.
The documentary covers Kahan's life, music career, and mental health journey over a year and a half.
Noah Kahan's fourth studio album, 'The Great Divide,' will be released on April 24.

title: Jon Keller, longtime WBZ political analyst, returns to TV
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Jon Keller, laid off last fall, returns to WBZ-TV as a 'special contributor' starting this week.
The 'Keller At Large' interview show, launched in 1991, resumes Sunday with Gov. Maura Healey as the guest.
Keller, a 12-time Emmy winner, was laid off when Skydance Media bought WBZ's parent company, Paramount.

title: ‘A relic of a bygone era’: Here’s why readers want Massachusetts to stop changing its clocks
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Over 83% of Boston.com readers support ending Massachusetts' biannual clock change, favoring a permanent time system.
Senate President Karen E. Spilka backs a bill to adopt Atlantic Standard Time if two other New England states agree.
Concerns include health impacts and the disruption of routines; opponents worry about darker winter mornings and regional coordination.

title: Impaired Stoneham teen drove 118 mph, fled police, and then crashed, N.H. authorities say
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: An 18-year-old from Stoneham was arrested for driving 118 mph and evading police in New Hampshire.
The teenager crashed a 2014 Chrysler 300 into a retaining wall on Route 101 after a pursuit.
He faces charges including felony reckless conduct and aggravated DUI, and was held in Rockingham County Jail.

title: The ‘World’s Shortest St. Paddy’s Day Parade’ is still New England’s nuttiest St. Pat’s fever dream
author: Lauren Daley
bullets: The 'World's Shortest St. Paddy's Day Parade' in Little Compton, Rhode Island is 89 feet long and lasted 40 minutes.
Approximately 2,000 attendees participated in the parade, which featured costumes, music, and a guest appearance by Matt Light.
The parade started as a joke in 2022, now supports local food banks and is included in Little Compton’s 350th Anniversary celebrations.

title: Massachusetts ER doctors say long wait times reflect a health system under strain
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Massachusetts ER doctors attribute long wait times to bed shortages, aging population, and gaps in primary care.
Patients in Massachusetts ERs spend an average of 189 minutes before leaving, placing the state among the highest for delays.
ER boarding occurs when patients wait for inpatient beds due to lack of space, worsening post-COVID-19 staffing issues.

title: Here’s what meteorologists are saying about heavy rain, damaging winds on Monday
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Heavy rainfall and thunderstorms could lead to localized flooding in Boston area, with 1-3 inches of rain expected.
National Weather Service issues Flood Watch, High Wind Warnings, and Wind Advisories for Monday due to severe weather.
Winds may reach gusts of 45-65 mph, particularly in eastern Massachusetts, posing risk of damage.

title: Firefighter suffers cardiac arrest during Hyde Park blaze
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: A firefighter suffered cardiac arrest during a warehouse fire on Sunday night in Hyde Park, Boston.
The blaze at 1 Westinghouse Plaza prompted a second alarm and caused $750,000 in damage.
The cause of the fire is under investigation by the Boston Fire Investigation Unit with no other injuries reported.
